title: adenocarpus complicatus subsp. graecus
intro: Adenocarpus complicatus subsp. graecus is a subspecies of the Adenocarpus complicatus plant, commonly known as Greek broom or mountain broom, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Greece, and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Adenocarpus complicatus subsp. graecus include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The plant can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
